title: The Contemporary World is an American Creation
content:
The contemporary world is largely an American creation, shaped significantly in the aftermath of World War II. During this period, the United States erected globe-spanning alliances and trade networks to build a liberal international order. This ambitious system fostered unprecedented prosperity and peace, bringing immense benefits by expanding global wealth and democratic ideals. However, like all heroic achievements, this narrative contains its share of myths and exaggerations. In reality, Washington sometimes secured this liberal order with illiberal expedients, such as military interventions or flouting its own rules.

Despite the inevitable hypocrisy and coercion, the American-led system mostly wielded its remarkable power to maintain global stability. Yet, there are growing signs that this old world order is currently deteriorating. Geopolitical challengers like China have actively worked to exploit the system from within. Beijing used its insertion into the global economy to build the massive influence it now uses to challenge the United States. Meanwhile, Washington itself has become fatally disillusioned with its own creation.

This domestic ambivalence stems from real problems, including persistent economic imbalances and the perception of free-riding among U.S. allies. Consequently, it increasingly seems that the established U.S.-led global structure is fracturing. Recent administrations have pushed Eurasian allies to take charge of their own defense while ruthlessly enforcing Washington's influence in the Americas. This shift and occasional disregard for international law echo older eras of aggressive regional dominance. Ultimately, we are now glimpsing the possibility that a fragmented world of hemispheric primacy could once again become the norm.
